objective: Determine which waveform families are best for the effective |f,n-1> <-> |g,n> sideband interaction in the local simulator, separating selective control from fastest practical unselective control for both storage-mode and readout-mode sidebands while making the cQED parameter provenance and transmon-decoherence assumptions explicit, and extend the study to test whether a simultaneous two-tone storage-sideband plus readout-sideband drive can rapidly transfer a storage photon into the readout mode under the local device parameters.
hypotheses: Smooth-edge pulses will outperform square pulses in the selective regime because they suppress high-frequency spectral weight that drives neighboring sideband manifolds.
Square or nearly square pulses will remain strongest contenders in the fastest unselective regime because they maximize effective Rabi rate for a fixed duration.
Open-system replay will make the practical winner set more restrictive than the closed-system winner set, especially for the readout sideband.
A resonant simultaneous two-tone drive will produce a bright-state single-photon storage-to-readout transfer on a tens-of-nanoseconds timescale.
A detuned simultaneous two-tone drive will reduce intermediate-state population but become more vulnerable to readout decay because of the slower effective exchange.
assumptions: The local sideband-reset example provides the authoritative Hamiltonian and mode-noise parameters.
The effective sideband-control abstraction is acceptable for waveform ranking within the current environment.
Low-lying manifolds n=1,2,3 are sufficient for the main comparison.
The matched local tomography workflow provides a reasonable transmon-decoherence sensitivity anchor because the sideband-reset example does not export transmon T1/T2.
The simultaneous two-tone transfer experiment is primarily a single-photon protocol study; n>1 cases are treated as diagnostics because the constant drive opens a longer conversion chain.

success_criteria.analytic: State the minimal effective sideband model, matrix-element scaling, and the expected speed-selectivity tradeoff from first principles before relying on numerics.
success_criteria.numerical: Generate mode-resolved waveform rankings, calibrated durations, leakage/selectivity metrics, gate-oriented diagnostics, and open-system summaries from full multilevel simulator replay.
success_criteria.outputs: Save scripts, figures, machine-readable summaries, a markdown report, a LaTeX report/PDF, and a reproducibility notebook.
success_criteria.validation: Demonstrate sanity checks, convergence, and an honest scope-limited comparison to the sideband literature where applicable.

completed_tasks: Initialized the study and run directories.
Created the README, improvements log, state file, and task-run planning documents.
Implemented the shared sideband-study helpers and waveform sweep runner.
Executed the storage/readout waveform comparison and generated the baseline figures and machine-readable outputs.
Exported an explicit device-parameter provenance table and noise-scenario table.
Added transmon-decoherence support to the study noise wrapper and executed the mode-only and transmon-inclusive open-system follow-up.
Added a gate-oriented ranking artifact and a transmon-reference reranking pass over the shortlist.
Validated the frequency formulas, timestep convergence, and truncation convergence.
Implemented and executed the simultaneous two-tone storage-to-readout transfer extension.
Validated the selected single-photon two-tone cases against the reduced three-state model, timestep convergence, and truncation convergence.
Updated the markdown report, LaTeX report/PDF, reproducibility notebook, and handoff files to reflect the extended conclusions.

two_tone_multi_photon_diagnostic.interpretation: Constant simultaneous driving opens a longer conversion chain for n>1, so the clean single-photon reduced-ladder picture does not generalize.

compute_notes.plan_budget: Main sweep estimated at roughly 2-6 minutes on CPU if the area-based calibration prior keeps the search focused.
